titleStmt.title: Letter from Philip C. MacDermot to Henry Arthur Wynne, 29 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Philip C. MacDermot

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Philip MacDermot (b. 1863), the crown solicitor for Roscommon, to Sir Henry Arthur Wynne referring to a case of cattle driving off 'Mathers Lands' in Ballinasloe. According to the letter one of the participants in the drive, Harvey, has not yet been arrested. Wynne does not see this as a problem as Harvey is a 'tramp labourer' who seems to merely have joined in the drive with the crowd.Wynne (1867 – 1945) was appointed to the vacant position of Chief Crown Solicitor following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
OFFICE OF CROWN SOLICITOR FOR CO. ROSCOMMON BOYLE. 29th July 1916
Cattle Driving off Mather lands.
Ballinasloe Prosecutions Dear Sir I am in receipt of your letter of yesterday's date.
From what the district inspector writes to me about Harvey, it will
not matter much if the police are not are not able to arrest him, as he is
a tramp Labourer and he is in no way interested in the dispute, and
appears (the District Inspector tells me) to have merely joined
in the Cattle Drive with the crowd, but probably he may be found
before next Saturday. I note that I am to send Brief to Mr John Cusack, I am
writing to him telling him that he will get it in a few days,
probably by Tuesday's post. The District Inspector has the file
of evidence, I have telegraphed to him to return it to me toâday
and I shall set to having it briefed on Monday as you are aware
it is rather long and probably I will not have it finished until
Tuesday. What fee is Mr Cusack to get? I expected a telegram from the District Inspector as to what
was done at Ballinasloe toâday, but I have not heard from him so
far. Since writing this for telegram from the D.I. the cases were adjourned to 5th
August. Pat Murray too ill to attend. Harvey has been arrested
& his case also adjourned: all out on bail Yours truly Philip C. MacDermot Chief Crown Solicitor, Dublin Castle
